text: The video visually demonstrates a single-port ileocolic resection with mesenterectomy, showing the surgical approach and technique used to remove the ileocolic segment while performing mesenterectomy.
The surgeon begins by creating a submesenteric tunnel, carefully dissecting beneath the mesentery to establish a pathway for the resection.
Following the creation of the submesenteric tunnel, the surgeon proceeds to take the lateral peritoneal attachments, facilitating the lateral mobilization of the bowel.
The surgeon marks the proximal transaction line on the bowel, preparing for the next step in the ileocolic resection.
The surgeon identifies the ileocolic trunk, ensuring it is spared during the extended mesenterectomy, with careful attention to preserving this vascular structure.
The surgeon identifies the position of the ileocolic trunk and proceeds to create a mesenteric window, ensuring the trunk is spared during the extended mesenterectomy.
The surgeon carefully dissects the mesentery away from the ileocolic trunk, ensuring the trunk is spared during the procedure.
The surgeon dissects the mesentery towards the marked proximal transaction point, ensuring careful separation of tissue along the intended surgical path.
The surgeon continues the dissection alongside the ileocolic trunk, carefully preserving this structure during the extended mesenterectomy.
The surgeon continues dissecting the mesentery towards the proximal transaction point, maintaining clear separation from the ileocolic trunk.
The surgeon continues the dissection alongside the ileocolic trunk, progressing towards the colonic transaction point, carefully separating the mesentery from surrounding structures.
The video displays the dissected triangular-shaped mesentery, highlighting its distinct shape and orientation within the surgical field.
The surgeon closes the mesenteric defect using clips after completing the extracorporeal anastomosis, ensuring the defect is securely sealed.
